mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, which compresses earnings multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the trigger fails to predict downward price momentum within 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer credit outstanding and new housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and expanding household balance sheets, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60d returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production within a supply-constrained macro environment signals firms are successfully passing higher input costs to consumers, expanding profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative SPX returns over 20 days in more than 50% of observations, or the series shows no predictive power for forward equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month inflation forces market participants to price in hawkish Federal Reserve policy, increasing the equity risk premium and discount rates, which compresses valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI acceleration does not lead to negative 5d forward returns, or the signal generates a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for public corporations, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no predictive power for 60d SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative forward returns in the majority of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction and wage-price spiral risks, signaling stagflation that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 20d returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing market activity in a low-rate environment signals rising consumer confidence and wealth effects, which boosts near-term equity demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not correlate with positive 5d SPX returns in low-rate periods, or the series exhibits zero correlation with forward equity momentum.
